description_text:
Data Entry Analyst
📍 Location: France (On-site / Hybrid / Remote options available)
We are seeking a detail-oriented Data Entry Analyst to support data management and reporting activities. This role is ideal for candidates who are comfortable working with data, have strong attention to detail, and are looking to grow their career in analytics or operations.
Key Responsibilities
Enter, update, and maintain accurate data across internal systems
Validate data quality and perform regular checks for accuracy and consistency
Support basic data analysis and reporting tasks
Assist in preparing dashboards, summaries, and operational reports
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure data integrity
Follow data protection and confidentiality guidelines
Requirements
Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data, Finance, or a related field
0–2 years of experience in data entry, data analysis, or administrative roles
Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy
Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP are a plus)
Ability to work independently and meet deadlines
English required; French is an advantage
What We Offer
Stable role with clear processes and training
Opportunity to gain hands-on experience with data and reporting
Supportive team environment
Competitive compensation based on experience
Long-term career development opportunities
📩 Apply directly on LinkedIn or submit your CV for review.

title: Group HR Data Analyst F/M
linkedin_org_slogan: The business community for LGBTQ+ professionals, graduates, inclusive employers and advocates for workplace equality.
linkedin_org_locations: 57 Southwark Street, London, England SE1 1RU, GB
linkedin_org_description:
myGwork is the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. 
Our mission is to make the workplace more inclusive for all by providing individual users and partner organizations access to a wide eco-system of services, including job opportunities, training, mentoring, employer branding, and free community events. Joining the platform is free for individual members, which supports myGwork’s goal of ensuring that the platform's benefits are as accessible and as far-reaching as possible. Corporate members get a tailored service, with carefully curated product packages to help them achieve all their DE&I goals.

myGwork organizes two annual events, WorkFair – the largest virtual global career fair for the LGBTQ+ professionals, graduates and students, and WorkPride – a week-long global conference for the LGBTQ+ business community and allies during Pride Month. The company also recently launched the myGwork Academy, delivering practical and relevant LGBTQ+ training education to help create inclusive workplaces for all. 

For more information, check out www.mygwork.com.
description_text:
This job is with Allianz Commercial, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly. 

The role

Reporting to Group HR Data Lead, the Group HR Data Analyst is responsible for steering the HR performance and oversees all KPIs activities related to Human resources. As part of the central team you will contribute to high quality planning, forecasting and tracking of relevant HR information, systems and applications.

Main missions

Operational HR Reporting

Group recurring reports at Allianz Trade (Monthly/Quarterly analysis, Board Reportings, Dashboards)
Adhoc reports from internal & external stakeholders
Analysing data and dashboards creation

HR Data Quality

Perform and manage regular HR Data quality checks and follow up with regional teams
Adhoc HR data quality checks (according to period)
Build clear processes to improve HR Data quality in systems

HR Reporting Project

Being part of group reporting process : CSRD (Corporate Social Responsibility Directives)
Automatization and rationalisation of reporting (at convenience with Power Query, Macro...)
Contribute in the HR Data Governance framework

Stakeholder Collaboration

Partner closely with HR Business Partners, Finance, IT and cross‑functional teams to optimise reporting toools, improve data quality and enhance the overall user experience
Support Regional and Local HR Controllers in mastering reporting tools, analytics methodologies and global standards

Knowledge / Experience / Skills Required

4 minimum years working experience either in HR data management, IT or Reporting
Excellent organization skills
Strong customer and service orientation
Very good communication and presentation skills
Mastery in Excel and proficiency in SAP (BW)
Solid internal control/audit experience 
Former experience in HR controlling
Comfortable in Matrix environment

#Paris

#France

Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. Caring for our employees, their ambitions, dreams and challenges, is what makes us a unique employer. Together we can build an environment where everyone feels empowered and has the confidence to explore, to grow and to shape a better future for our customers and the world around us.

At Allianz, we stand for unity: we believe that a united world is a more prosperous world, and we are dedicated to consistently advocating for equal opportunities for all. And the foundation for this is our inclusive workplace, where people and performance both matter, and nurtures a culture grounded in integrity, fairness, inclusion and trust.

We therefore welcome applications regardless of ethnicity or cultural background, age, gender, nationality, religion, social class, disability or sexual orientation, or any other characteristics protected under applicable local laws and regulations.

Join us. Let's care for tomorrow.

]]>

title: Data Security & DLP Analyst
linkedin_org_slogan: Where experts get paid to train AI.
linkedin_org_description: We're looking for experts to help train better AI. At Alignerr, we offer paid, flexible projects for writers, coders, and subject matter experts to refine and align advanced artificial intelligence. Work when you want, where you want. Apply today at Alignerr.com or through our open Job Postings.
description_text:
About The Job

At Alignerr, we partner with the world’s leading AI research teams and labs to build and train cutting-edge AI models.

We’re looking for people who understand how data leaks actually happen. You will work with realistic incidents and controls to model how sensitive information is exposed, detected, and protected across modern organizations.

Organization: Alignerr Position: Data Security & DLP Analyst Type: Contract / Task-Based Compensation: $38–$60 /hour Location: Remote Commitment: 10–40 hours/week

What You’ll Do

Analyze data-security and DLP scenarios across cloud, SaaS, and enterprise systems 
Classify data sensitivity, exposure paths, and policy violations 
Review prevention, detection, and response strategies 
Help generate, label, and validate realistic data-security cases used to train and evaluate AI systems 

What We’re Looking For

2+ years in data security, compliance, or security operations 
Familiarity with DLP tools, data classification, and privacy or regulatory concepts 
Practical understanding of how data risk plays out in real environments 

Why Join Us

Competitive pay and flexible remote work. 
Work directly on frontier AI systems. 
Freelance perks: autonomy, flexibility, and global collaboration. 
Potential for contract extension. 

Application Process (Takes 10–15 min)

Submit your resume 
Complete a short screening 
Project matching and onboarding 

PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.

title: DATA ANALYST (H/F)
linkedin_org_slogan: La DATA au service de la performance.
linkedin_org_locations: 99, Avenue Aristide Briand, Montrouge, Île-de-France 92120, FR
linkedin_org_description:
Nous accompagnons nos clients tout au long de leurs réflexions et projets de pilotage de la performance.

Nous sommes spécialisés dans le pilotage de la performance et des systèmes de business intelligence. Nos prestations: définition et cadrage des projets décisionnels, assistance à la maîtrise d'ouvrage, intégration et déploiement des solutions.

Notre proposition de services couvre tous les étapes d'un projet décisionnel autour de 3 offres principales : 
•	cadrage de projet, 
•	accompagnement MOA/MOE, 
•	pilotage des programmes business intelligence,
•	conception, intégration et maintenance des solutions décisionnelles.

Nous avons développé une expertise dans les domaines de:
•	convergence du SI décisionnel d'entreprise
•	pilotage financier et opérationnel
•	intégration des données non-structurées
•	analyse et intégration des médias sociaux

Nos valeurs phares, notre « marque de fabrique » :
•	Le professionnalisme de nos consultants qui font bénéficier nos clients de leurs expériences dans la définition et la mise en place de solutions de pilotage.
•	Le pragmatisme focalisé sur la création de valeur et sur des résultats concrets.
•	L’innovation: connaissance des offres décisionnelles les plus récentes et le recul sur le marché de la Business Intelligence pour proposer à nos clients, en toute impartialité, les meilleures solutions.
•	La souplesse et la réactivité de nos prestations, assurées par notre structure à taille humaine et l’implication du management.
•	L'écoute, base fondamentale de l’accompagnement de nos clients.
description_text:
LOBELLIA Conseil et ASTILLIA forment un groupe composé d’une centaine de consultants.

LOBELLIA Conseil est un cabinet de conseil et de service spécialisé dans la valorisation de la donnée : Business Intelligence, Big Data et EPM. Elle accompagne ses clients tout au long de leurs projets DATA et EPM, sur les réflexions en amont des projets et sur l’exécution tant en Assistance à Maitrise d’Ouvrage qu’en Maitrise d’Œuvre. LOBELLIA Conseil est partenaire GOLD JEDOX.

LOBELLIA Conseil intervient pour des clients grands comptes sur des secteurs variés (finances, industrie, services, secteur public, ).. Celles-ci s articulent depuis les études de cadrage jusqu’ à l accompagnement des métiers dans la prise en main des solutions de pilotage et l’implémentation de solutions Data dans des architectures Cloud et/ou on-premise.

LOBELLIA Conseil regroupe au sein de ses équipes, des consultants passionnés par la Data de différents profils (Data Architect, Business Analyst Data, Data Analyst, Data Engineer, …).

Nos collaborateurs interviennent toujours en équipe dédiée en mode projet chez nos clients.

La flexibilité et la réactivité de notre structure à taille humaine alliées à notre expertise forte sur des projets d envergure sont les clefs de la réussite de notre croissance.

ASTILLIA est une société qui conçoit et développe des Logiciels spécialisés sur un métier. Elle porte l offre DIGITALE du groupe.

LOBELLIA Conseil recherche plus particulièrement des Consultant(e)s AMOA/Product Owner/Business Analyst afin d’intervenir auprès de différents clients grands comptes, dans le but de les accompagner dans leur démarche de transformation data et digitale.

Vos missions
Nos postes sont très variés. Nous ne recrutons pas pour une mission, nous recrutons un profil ! Notre vision n’est pas tournée vers le court terme, car nous avons à cœur de vous faire grandir aussi bien professionnellement, que personnellement !

Bien évidemment, si vous êtes sur cette offre, c’est que vous souhaitez en savoir plus.

Voici donc les travaux que vous pourriez être amené à réaliser :

– Animation d’ateliers d’expression de besoin avec les directions Métiers ou IT

– Conception de la solution et rédaction de spécifications fonctionnelles

– Accompagnement des utilisateurs dans le maquettage de leurs Dashboards (Dataviz) et réalisation de Dashboards en lien étroit avec les métiers,

– Gestion du lien entre les besoins métiers et l’équipe de mise en œuvre,

– Préparation, organisation et exécution des tests de qualification de l’ensemble de la solution : rédaction des cahiers de recette et support à la recette utilisateur,

– Organisation et exécution des actions de conduite de changement

– Pilotage et coordination des actions des différents acteurs Projet (MOA et MOE): suivi des actions, consolidation et reporting, suivi des jalons, suivi des développements.

Vous interviendrez ainsi sur les différentes phases projet, de conception, développement, validation et déploiement de la solution.

Afin d’accompagner le développement de l’agence lyonnaise, vous serez aussi amenés à participer à nos publications et à l’activité commerciale de l’agence.

Nous sommes une entreprise à taille humaine qui accompagne activement ses consultants. Vous bénéficierez ainsi de toute l’expertise de LOBELLIA Conseil en termes de conduite et de méthodologie de définition et construction de solutions décisionnelles. Dans ce cadre, vous serez formé et encadré sur l’utilisation de différents outils de Business Intelligence.

Vos compétences
Nous recherchons des candidats ayant acquis au moins 5 années d’expérience dans un cabinet de conseil ou SSII sur des sujets data, notamment en tant que Data Analyst, Consultant AMOA / Business Analyst.

– Diplôme BAC + 5 (école d’ingénieur ou universitaire), vous êtes passionné par la Data et souhaitez progresser dans la maîtrise des outils BI, de Dataviz et dans le pilotage de projet

– Vous êtes à l’aise sur les méthodologies projet classiques et agiles et vous possédez une connaissance de l’architecture d’un SI Data (BI, Big Data).

– Vous connaissez une solution de Dataviz de type Power BI ou Tableau Software ainsi que SQL

– Vous avez un bon esprit d’analyse et de synthèse une très bonne capacité rédactionnelle et relationnelle,

– Vous êtes parfaitement à l’aise en anglais.

title: Data Analyst / Data Scientist
linkedin_org_slogan: Bridging the gap to tomorrow with cutting-edge digital solutions.
linkedin_org_description:
Wasla is a digital solutions company based in Egypt. We help businesses scale by designing and building web, mobile, and enterprise products.

Our work spans strategy, design, and full‑stack development, bringing world‑class software and digital platforms to life. With a focused team and a drive for excellence, we deliver technology built for growth and built to last.
description_text:
Data Analyst / Data Scientist
📍 Location: France (On-site / Hybrid / Remote options available)
We are looking for a Data Analyst / Data Scientist to support data-driven decision-making through analysis, modeling, and insights generation. This role is ideal for candidates with a strong analytical mindset who enjoy working with data to solve real-world problems.
Key Responsibilities
Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Develop reports, dashboards, and data visualizations
Build predictive models and perform statistical analysis (for more advanced tasks)
Identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support business decisions
Maintain data quality and documentation
Requirements
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
1–3 years of experience in data analysis or data science roles
Strong skills in Excel, SQL, and at least one programming language (Python or R)
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau)
Knowledge of statistical methods or machine learning techniques is a plus
Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking skills
Good communication skills in English
French is an advantage but not required
What We Offer
Opportunity to work with real-world data and impactful projects
Exposure to both analytical and data science tasks
Collaborative and data-driven environment
Career development opportunities in analytics and AI
Competitive compensation and flexible working arrangements
📩 Apply via LinkedIn or submit your CV for consideration.

title: Senior Data Analyst  - Internal Audit
linkedin_org_slogan: Helping industries outrun with our leading technologies in electrification and automation. go.abb/outrun
linkedin_org_description:
ABB is a technology leader in electrification and automation, enabling a more sustainable and resource-efficient future. The company’s solutions connect engineering know-how and software to optimize how things are manufactured, moved, powered and operated. Building on over 140 years of excellence, ABB’s more than 110,000 employees are committed to driving innovations that accelerate industrial transformation.  www.abb.com

Privacy notice & house rules: https://social.abb/social-media-information

Before applying for a job at ABB, please read our Fraud Warning: https://careers.abb/global/en/fraud-warning
description_text:
At ABB, we help industries outrun - leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation - for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world.

This role sits within ABB's Robotics business, a leading global robotics company. We're entering an exciting new chapter as we’ve announced the plan for SoftBank Group to acquire ABB Robotics. SoftBank is a globally recognized technology group and investor/operator focused on AI, robotics, and next-generation computing. By joining us now, you’ll be part of a pioneering team shaping the future of robotics—working alongside world-class experts in a fast-moving, innovation-driven environment.

This Position reports to:

Senior Project Finance Manager

In this role, you will support the development of a data-driven Internal Audit function by leveraging analytics, automation, and visualization to enhance audit effectiveness and insights. You will contribute to building modern audit capabilities while supporting risk management, operational excellence, and governance across a global organization. Working closely with Internal Audit stakeholders, you will transform data into actionable insights, enabling more informed decision-making and strengthening audit processes.

The work model for the role is: hybrid;

The location of this position is within Europe.

Your responsibilities:

Data Analytics & Audit Support: Conduct data analysis to identify anomalies, trends, and outliers, supporting audit activities and ad hoc internal audit projects.
Tools & Infrastructure Development: Support the development of Internal Audit data analytics infrastructure, including reusable data models, scripts, automated workflows, and analytics tools.
Data Management & Integrity: Ensure data accuracy, completeness, and integrity through reconciliations and validation processes.
Visualization & Reporting: Create interactive dashboards and data visualizations, and enhance audit and management reports using clear visual storytelling techniques.
Continuous Improvement & Innovation: Contribute to building a modern, data-enabled Internal Audit function by developing, testing, and optimizing data analysis solutions.

Your background:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Engineering, Statistics, Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
Minimum of 10 years of relevant professional experience in data analytics within a business environment (e.g., Finance, Internal Audit, Controlling, or similar functions)
Strong Excel skills, including advanced formulas, pivot tables, and data modeling capabilities.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Familiarity with data analytics and preparation tools (e.g., Alteryx, ACL Analytics, Python).
Strong communication skills in English, with high attention to detail and strong organizational abilities.
Proficiency in PowerPoint and a proactive, collaborative mindset with the ability to work in a fast-paced, international environment.

What’s in it for you?

We empower you to take initiative, challenge ideas, and lead with confidence. You’ll grow through meaningful work, continuous learning, and support that’s tailored to your goals. Every idea you share and every action you take contributes to something bigger.

We value people from different backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Data Quality Analyst (French) | $11/hr Remote
linkedin_org_slogan: Connecting ambitious talent with high-growth companies worldwide.
linkedin_org_description:
Crossing Hurdles connects skilled professionals with opportunities across leading AI training platforms and high-growth companies.

As global demand for human input in AI development continues to grow, many platforms rely on large networks of capable contributors across technical, analytical, and knowledge-based domains. Crossing Hurdles helps professionals discover and access these opportunities by bringing together information, talent networks, and application pathways related to AI model training, evaluation, and emerging AI-enabled work.

Through our growing community of candidates and professionals, we help individuals explore opportunities with global AI platforms that are building and improving next-generation AI systems. These opportunities often span areas such as technical analysis, research, reasoning tasks, and other knowledge-driven work that supports the development of modern AI models.

Alongside the AI ecosystem, Crossing Hurdles also works with high-growth companies to support talent discovery across key business functions. We collaborate with founders, leadership teams, and hiring managers to help them connect with capable professionals for full-time roles across both on-site and remote teams.

Over the past few quarters, we have partnered with companies including Angel One, Ixigo, Cars24, Veera, ABP Network, Battery Smart, Zavya, and Twin Engineers, supporting hiring initiatives across multiple sectors.

Key areas of focus
• Opportunities across AI training and evaluation platforms
• Technology & Product roles
• Growth, Marketing & Sales
• Customer Success & Support
• Finance & Business Operations

By bringing together talent networks, industry insights, and emerging opportunities across AI and high-growth companies, Crossing Hurdles aims to help capable professionals discover meaningful work while supporting organizations in reaching the talent they need to grow.
description_text:
Position: LLM – AI Quality Analyst (Personalization) – French

Type: Short-Term Contract

Location: Remote

Commitment: 20 hours/week (with PST overlap)

Engagement Length: 1 month

Start Date: Immediate

Role Responsibilities

Design multi-turn conversational prompts based on personal context
Evaluate personalized AI responses for relevance, grounding, and helpfulness
Assess correct and incorrect use of personal data in model outputs
Perform side-by-side (SxS) evaluation and ranking of AI responses
Identify grounding errors, poor inferences, and forced personalization
Write clear, structured rationales referencing specific conversation turns
Extract and verify model debug information and data source usage
Maintain strict data hygiene by deleting evaluation conversations

Requirements

French fluency (reading and writing)
Strong experience in data annotation, AI quality evaluation, content moderation, or related roles
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Ability to evaluate nuanced and ambiguous AI responses
Comfortable using a primary personal Google account with enabled data sources
BS/BA degree or equivalent experience in a relevant analytical field
Strong written communication and structured feedback skills
Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote setting
Reliable desktop/laptop with stable internet connection

Application Process

Fill out the application form
Complete the ICF
Assessment
